paragraphs: I'm writing to apply for the Senior Engineer (C# .Net) role you're recruiting for on behalf of your financial services client. My background is a close match for this opening: over 7 years of cumulative technical experience building and supporting enterprise C#/.NET applications, with recent work sitting squarely in regulated financial-adjacent domains — insurance and audit/advisory — alongside the AWS cloud infrastructure and CI/CD discipline the role calls for.

At Delta Insurance, I own the release lifecycle for both a legacy .NET Framework platform and a .NET Core microservices ecosystem deployed on AWS EC2, including code reviews, release scheduling, and CI/CD via GitHub Actions and AWS CodePipeline/CodeCommit — while also reviewing peer Senior Developers' architectural and code contributions to keep the systems aligned with SOLID principles. Earlier, at Punongbayan & Araullo, I built and optimized SQL Server and Oracle PL/SQL stored procedures and complex queries supporting the firm's audit and advisory operations, using Azure DevOps for source control, code reviews, and CI/CD execution — direct experience with the enterprise-scale, financial-domain application work this role centers on.

I haven't worked directly with Java, post-trade affirmation/confirmation messaging, or FIX/Solace/Tibco, so I want to be upfront about that rather than overstate it. What I bring instead is a strong C#/.NET foundation, hands-on SQL Server and cloud/CI-CD experience in financial-adjacent enterprise systems, and a track record of ramping quickly on new stacks and domains across five roles in five years.
I'd welcome the chance to discuss how my background fits this position and am available to start on short notice. Thank you for your consideration.
